page: Amazon Web Services m5ad.4xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor. This x86_64 architecture CPU features 16 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) mapped to 8 dedicated physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 2.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes a 600 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively low, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10% tier. However, multi-core execution shows moderate capability, with Geekbench multi-core, software compilation, and ray tracing benchmarks achieving average performance in the middle 50% tier. Memory bandwidth is average for small cached blocks at 620.66 GB/sec, but drops to weak levels for larger uncached blocks. Database and web serving operations perform steadily, with Redis SET operations reaching 10.02 million operations per second and static web serving throughput averaging 6.2 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5ad.4xlarge utilizes previous-generation AMD EPYC hardware and DDR4 memory, offering a balanced resource density of compute, memory, and high-speed local NVMe storage without the premium of newer architectures. The inclusion of 600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage makes it suitable for workloads requiring temporary high-speed scratch space. This server is appropriate for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations that can leverage the local instance store and do not require high single-core CPU performance.
description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.4xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features a 600 GB local NVMe SSD instance store and a 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results indicate weak single-core performance, but multi-core capabilities, software compilation, and ray tracing fall within the average tier. Memory bandwidth is average for small cached blocks but drops for larger uncached blocks. With 10.02 million operations per second on Redis SET benchmarks and 6.2 GB/sec static web serving throughput, this instance is appropriate for database operations, multi-threaded applications, and web serving. It offers a balanced resource profile for workloads that benefit from local NVMe storage but do not demand top-tier single-threaded CPU speeds.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.4xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 600 GB local NVMe SSD, offering balanced multi-core performance for databases and web serving.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor
64.0 GB of DDR4 memory paired with a 600 GB local NVMe SSD
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Average multi-core performance for software compilation and database operations
Appropriate for web serving, multi-threaded applications, and temporary storage workloads
